name: h3 (Minimal HTTP Framework for Edge/Node)

auth.notes: HTTP framework utilities; auth via middleware

agent_readiness.notes: Minimal HTTP framework powering Nitro, Nuxt 3, Analog. defineEventHandler(), readBody(), getQuery(), sendRedirect(), setResponseHeader(). Adapters for Node.js, Bun, Cloudflare Workers, Deno, AWS Lambda, Vercel, Netlify. EventHandler pattern replaces req/res. Composable utilities (event handlers). No routing built-in (use with Nitro/unrouted). TypeScript-native. setHeader(), getHeader(), deleteCookie(), setCookie() utilities. useBase() for path prefixing. Middleware via createRouter(). Foundation of Nuxt 3's server engine.

gotchas: No routing — needs Nitro or similar for routing
Less opinionated than Hono/Express — more assembly required
Primarily used through Nitro/Nuxt rather than directly
best_when: Nuxt 3 server routes (h3 is underlying framework)
Edge-first TypeScript APIs across multiple runtimes
Nitro-based projects and UnJS ecosystem users
Minimal HTTP layer with maximum portability
avoid_when: Need full-featured framework (use Hono or Fastify)
summary: h3 is the minimal HTTP event handler library powering Nuxt 3, Nitro, and the UnJS ecosystem. defineEventHandler() with adapters for Node.js, Bun, Cloudflare Workers, Deno, Lambda. TypeScript-native with utility functions for body parsing, cookies, headers. Foundation for edge-compatible TypeScript server code. Used directly in Nuxt 3 server routes. Use Hono or Fastify for standalone full-featured HTTP frameworks.
